title: The Last Appointment
description: In The Vertex there is something that feels like waiting. It's near the traveler but not quite there. If the Fool Card carries all five marks, it has begun to pulse — gently, the way a thing pulses when it has been patient for six worlds and is almost allowed to stop.

offer.speaker: Madame Orrery
offer.text: You followed the pulse. Good. I wasn't sure I still registered as findable.

offer.speaker: Madame Orrery
offer.text: I remember why I started traveling. I was looking for someone who had left without saying goodbye. I've looked in six worlds. I've found many interesting things. I found you.

accept.speaker: Madame Orrery
accept.text: Sit with me a moment. The card in your pocket has five marks and one question left in it. So do I.

decline.speaker: Madame Orrery
decline.text: Then not yet. The waiting doesn't mind. The waiting is the one thing that has never minded.

in_progress.speaker: Madame Orrery
in_progress.text: Six worlds of notes to compare, and it turns out the only entry that matters is the first one. It usually is.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Madame Orrery
ready_to_turn_in.text: Ask me if I'm ready. I've been asking travelers variations of that question my whole route. I would like to hear it pointed the other way.

complete.speaker: Madame Orrery
complete.text: I think I'll stop here for a while.

objectives.description: Follow the Fool Card's pulse to what has been waiting.
Sit with her. Hear the whole route.
Answer the question she has carried for six worlds.

cross_world_connection: Chain A finale, 6 of 6. The world6_orrery cutscene (fired at turn-in) is the chain's full payoff, already authored and fully dispatchable: the four-option choice climax ('Her voice doesn't expect an answer. It expects company.'), the Orrery Pendant grant, the Calibrant's true name spoken-not-spelled, and the Fool Card's five marks rearranging into the symbol from Phil's paper — fool_card -> wild_card via update_item. She sits down. In The Vertex, sitting is how things that are ending choose to continue.
_wiring_notes: DEDUP RULE: all grants (orrery_pendant) + the fool_card->wild_card update_item live in the world6_orrery cutscene; quest carries exp only. prereq_flag quest_wiring_fool_card_five_marks is a WIRING-OWNED boolean: emit when the fool_card_marks value flag reaches 5 (QuestSystem v1 prereqs are boolean; the marks counter is a value — one-line emitter wherever marks are set, or a QuestSystem check hook, wirer's choice). The giver 'NPC' is a presence, not a sprite — the Fool Card's pulse is the diegetic marker (if a visual is needed: a faint shimmer near-but-not-at the traveler, per the doc's 'near the traveler but not quite there'). Step 2 emitter: a sit-and-listen dialogue chain (2-3 beats, her route retold briefly). THE CHOICE (four responses, none wrong, silence included) lives INSIDE the cutscene per the shipped choice-step semantics — the quest deliberately does not model it; the doc's only fail-path (skipping the dialogue entirely = chain remains unresolved) maps to declining/never-completing the quest, which leaves her traveling: correct. If the player somehow reaches W6 without five marks, the presence never spawns and the chain resolves as 'she is still traveling' — acceptable incompleteness BY DESIGN (the completionist payoff is the payoff FOR completionists).
